name: Moscarter Lighthouse Loop
name_original: Faro des Moscarter desde Portinatx — circular trailrunning por el norte de Ibiza
description: Jog this moderate 3.8-mile (6.1 km) circular trail from Portinatx to the iconic Faro des Moscarter, enjoying stunning coastal views.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Sant Joan De Labritja traverse a diverse and unspoiled natural landscape in northern Ibiza. The region is characterized by mountainous areas, rugged cliffs, and dense pine forests, providing varied terrain for runners. Serene valleys, ancient olive groves, and a coastline dotted with coves and hidden beaches also define the area. This varied environment offers a wide array of running routes suitable for different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Sant Joan De Labritja The most popular jogging route is Moscarter Lighthouse Loop, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail that takes about 40 minutes to complete. This route offers coastal views and passes by the scenic Moscarter lighthouse. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from Portinatx, a moderate 3.8 miles (6.2 km) path. This route features varied terrain and views of the coastline around Portinatx. Local runners also love the View of Cala de Portinatx – Moscarter Lighthouse loop from Cala de Xuclar, a 5.3 miles (8.6 km) trail leading through coastal areas and offering views of Cala de Portinatx, often completed in about 57 minutes. Jogging around Sant Joan De Labritja is defined by coastal paths, dense pine forests, and serene valleys. The network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging trails. The routes in Sant Joan De Labritja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 30 reviews. Nearly 900 runners have used komoot to explore Sant Joan De Labritja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Sant Joan De Labritja?
A: The running routes in Sant Joan De Labritja offer a diverse and unspoiled natural landscape. You'll find mountainous areas, rugged cliffs, and dense pine forests. Many trails feature coastal views, serene valleys, and ancient olive groves. The terrain varies, with some paths being paved and others requiring more sure-footedness, accommodating all skill levels.

Q: Are there easy running routes for beginners or families in Sant Joan De Labritja?
A: Yes, Sant Joan De Labritja offers routes suitable for various fitness levels, including easier options. While the majority are moderate, there are 2 routes classified as easy. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000943836/moscarter-lighthouse-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moscarter Lighthouse Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular, moderate trail that is often enjoyed by many, and the region generally provides paths that are accessible for families looking for a gentle run.

Q: Where can I find more challenging running trails in the area?
A: For those seeking a greater challenge, Sant Joan De Labritja has 11 routes classified as difficult. These trails often traverse more rugged landscapes, dense forests, and hilly terrains, providing endurance challenges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31457698" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balansat Tower or des Molar loop from Port de Sant Miquel&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, features significant elevation gain, making it a good option for experienced runners.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions to see while running in Sant Joan De Labritja?
A: Absolutely! Many running routes offer scenic views and pass by interesting landmarks. You can spot the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/473420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moscarter Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/334642" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala d'en Serra&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/473511" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Portinatx&lt;/a&gt;, and take in panoramic views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3809407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ses Torretes Peak&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features unique natural caves like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5847493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova des Forn&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4521868" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Green pools and waterfall at Can Marçà Cave&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Sant Joan De Labritja?
A: While specific regulations for each trail may vary, the natural and unspoiled character of Sant Joan De Labritja generally makes it a welcoming area for outdoor activities with dogs. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near other people, and to clean up after them to preserve the natural environment.

Q: When is the best time of year to go running in Sant Joan De Labritja?
A: Sant Joan De Labritja offers pleasant running conditions for much of the year. The spring and autumn months are particularly ideal, with milder temperatures and lush landscapes. Even in summer, early morning or late afternoon runs can be enjoyable, especially along coastal paths where sea breezes provide some relief. Winters are generally mild, making it possible to run year-round.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Sant Joan De Labritja?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Sant Joan De Labritja are designed as loops, offering convenient circular options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39556424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Portinatx&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular path that allows you to start and finish in the same location, enjoying varied terrain and coastal views.

Q: How many running routes are there in Sant Joan De Labritja?
A: There are over 50 dedicated running routes available in Sant Joan De Labritja, offering a wide array of choices for different preferences and fitness levels. This includes a mix of easy, moderate, and challenging trails.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Sant Joan De Labritja?
A: The running routes in Sant Joan De Labritja are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 30 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, stunning coastal views, and the peaceful, unspoiled natural environment that allows for an authentic outdoor experience away from crowded spots.

Q: Is parking available near the running routes?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in the villages and near popular trailheads throughout Sant Joan De Labritja. Many routes, especially those starting from areas like Portinatx or Cala de Sant Vicent, have designated parking spots, making it convenient to access the trails by car.

Q: How accessible are the running trails by public transport?
A: While some villages and coastal areas in Sant Joan De Labritja are served by public transport, access to specific trailheads might require a short walk or a local taxi. It's advisable to check local bus schedules and routes in advance, especially if you plan to rely solely on public transport to reach the starting points of your chosen running routes.

Q: Are there any running routes around 10 km in length?
A: Yes, you can find several running routes that are approximately 10 km or longer. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429494" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cala de Portinatx – Moscarter Lighthouse loop from Cala de Xuclar&lt;/a&gt; is about 8.6 km, offering a substantial run with beautiful coastal scenery. The region also features longer trails like the Ruta de Balansat (PR-EI-106), which is an 18.82 km journey for those seeking extended distances.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to go trail running on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a great experience year-round, but for trail running, spring and autumn provide the most comfortable temperatures. During these seasons, you can enjoy the stunning coastal views without the intense summer heat, making for a more pleasant and invigorating run.

faqs.question: Is the Faro des Moscarter trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, due to rocky and uneven sections, especially near the lighthouse, and parts that run close to cliffs, it's essential that your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash for their safety and the safety of others. Ensure they are comfortable with varied terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to access the Faro des Moscarter trail from Portinatx. It's freely accessible for public enjoyment.

faqs.question: Where can I park when visiting the trailhead in Portinatx?
faqs.answer: Parking is available in Portinatx, which serves as the starting point for this circular trail. Look for designated parking areas within the town, as specific trailhead parking might be limited. Arriving earlier in the day, especially during peak seasons, can help secure a spot.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, offering a moderate challenge. You'll encounter a mix of mostly flat sections, but also rocky and uneven paths, particularly as you approach the lighthouse. It includes dedicated hiking paths, some street sections, and natural, unpaved surfaces. Be prepared for areas close to cliffs that require sure-footedness.

faqs.question: What notable sights will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This route offers stunning coastal views and passes by several points of interest. You'll get to see the iconic Moscarter Lighthouse, known for its distinctive black-and-white spiral. The trail also provides glimpses of secluded spots like Cala d'en Serra and the historic Torre de Portinatx, all set against a backdrop of rugged coastline and turquoise waters.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with young children?
faqs.answer: The trail is rated as moderate. While relatively short, its rocky paths and proximity to cliffs require good fitness and sure-footedness. For beginners, it's manageable if you take your time. However, caution is advised for families with young children due to the uneven terrain and cliffside sections.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the entire route follows the official trail known as Faro des Moscarter, providing a dedicated path for your trail running experience.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or amenities near the trailhead in Portinatx?
faqs.answer: Portinatx is a well-established resort town, so you'll find various amenities, including cafes, restaurants, and shops, especially near the main beach areas. These are convenient for refreshments or supplies before or after your trail run.

faqs.question: What is the best strategy for experiencing the coastal views on this trail?
faqs.answer: To fully appreciate the dramatic coastal scenery, consider starting your run earlier in the morning. This allows you to enjoy the cooler temperatures and the softer light, which beautifully illuminates the rugged coastline and the turquoise Mediterranean Sea, enhancing your views of landmarks like the Moscarter Lighthouse.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this trail running route?
faqs.answer: This circular trail running route is approximately 6.14 kilometers long and typically takes around 40 minutes to complete at a jogging pace. Your actual time may vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the views.
